name: Roasted Chana
preparation: Soak chana overnight and drain well.
Dry them thoroughly with a towel.
Toss with oil, salt, and chilli powder.
Spread on a baking tray and roast at 180°C for 25-30 minutes until crunchy.
Sprinkle chaat masala and let cool.
notes: Store in an airtight container for up to a week. They get crunchier as they cool.

name: Masala Chana
preparation: Boil kala chana until tender, drain.
Add chopped onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Sprinkle chaat masala and red chilli powder.
Squeeze lemon juice and toss well.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: A high-protein street-style chaat. Add cucumber for extra crunch.

name: Chana Jor Garam
preparation: Place chana jor in a bowl.
Add chopped onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Sprinkle chaat masala and squeeze lemon juice.
Toss well to combine.
Top with sev and coriander. Serve immediately.
notes: A classic Mumbai street snack. Best eaten immediately before the chana softens.

name: Spicy Roasted Chana
preparation: Drain and thoroughly dry soaked chana.
Toss with oil, chilli powder, garam masala, pepper, and salt.
Spread on baking tray.
Roast at 180°C for 30-35 minutes, tossing midway.
Cool completely for maximum crunch.
notes: Extra spicy version for those who love heat. Adjust chilli to taste.

name: Sweet Roasted Chana
preparation: Drain and dry soaked chana.
Toss with oil and spread on baking tray.
Roast at 180°C for 25 minutes.
While warm, toss with jaggery powder, cardamom, and salt.
Let cool completely.
notes: A sweet-savory treat. Jaggery adds minerals along with sweetness.

name: Roasted Makhana
preparation: Heat ghee in a pan on low flame.
Add makhana and roast on low heat for 8-10 minutes, stirring continuously.
They should become crisp and crunchy.
Add salt, pepper, and turmeric. Toss well.
Cool completely before storing.
notes: Makhana is a fasting-friendly snack. Roast on low heat to avoid burning.

name: Masala Makhana
preparation: Dry roast makhana in ghee on low heat for 8-10 minutes.
Remove from heat when crispy.
Sprinkle red chilli powder, chaat masala, cumin, and salt.
Toss well to coat evenly.
Cool and store in airtight container.
notes: Perfect tea-time snack. Rich in calcium and low in calories.

name: Cheese Makhana
preparation: Roast makhana in butter on low heat until crispy.
Remove from heat.
Immediately sprinkle grated cheese, oregano, and chilli flakes.
Toss quickly while warm so cheese sticks.
Let cool slightly and serve.
notes: A fusion makhana snack. Kids love this cheesy version.

name: Peri Peri Makhana
preparation: Roast makhana in oil on low heat until crispy.
Remove from heat.
Sprinkle peri peri seasoning and garlic powder.
Toss well to coat.
Cool and enjoy.
notes: A spicy, tangy twist on traditional makhana. Adjust peri peri to taste.

name: Caramel Makhana Light
preparation: Dry roast makhana in ghee until crispy. Set aside.
In the same pan, melt jaggery with water on low heat.
When bubbly, add cardamom and salt.
Add roasted makhana and toss quickly to coat.
Spread on parchment paper to cool and separate.
notes: A healthier alternative to caramel popcorn. Jaggery instead of refined sugar.

name: Sprout Chaat
preparation: Steam or boil mixed sprouts for 5-7 minutes until tender.
Drain and let cool slightly.
Add onion, tomato, green chilli.
Sprinkle chaat masala and squeeze lemon juice.
Top with coriander and sev. Serve immediately.
notes: High in protein and fiber. A healthy street-food-style snack.

name: Moong Sprout Salad
preparation: Lightly steam moong sprouts for 3-4 minutes.
Let cool to room temperature.
Add pomegranate, cucumber, and onion.
Season with lemon juice, chaat masala, and salt.
Garnish with coriander and serve.
notes: Pomegranate adds sweetness and antioxidants. Great for weight watchers.

name: Chana Sprout Chaat
preparation: Boil sprouted chana until tender (about 15 minutes).
Drain and cool.
Mix with chopped onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Add chaat masala, cumin, lemon juice, and salt.
Garnish with coriander.
notes: Sprouted chana is easier to digest and has higher nutrient availability.

name: Mixed Sprout Bowl
ingredients: 1 cup mixed sprouts (moong, moth, chana, masoor)
preparation: Steam mixed sprouts until tender.
Place in a bowl.
Top with whisked yogurt.
Drizzle tamarind and green chutney.
Sprinkle cumin, chaat masala, and top with sev.
notes: A protein-packed chaat bowl. The chutneys add sweet-tangy flavor.

name: Sprout Tikki
preparation: Mash boiled sprouts and potato together.
Add besan, cumin, chilli powder, and salt. Mix well.
Shape into small flat tikkis.
Heat oil on a non-stick pan.
Pan-fry tikkis on medium heat until golden on both sides.
notes: Serve with green chutney. Can be baked at 180°C for a healthier version.

name: Dhokla Snack
preparation: Mix besan, yogurt, water, sugar, lemon juice, and salt into smooth batter.
Add eno and mix quickly.
Pour into greased steaming plate.
Steam for 15-20 minutes until a toothpick comes clean.
Temper with mustard seeds, curry leaves, and chillies. Pour over dhokla.
notes: A classic Gujarati snack. Light, spongy, and healthy. Serve with green chutney.

name: Mini Dhokla
preparation: Mix besan, semolina, yogurt, water, and salt.
Add eno and pour into mini silicone moulds.
Steam for 10-12 minutes.
Prepare tadka with mustard seeds, sesame seeds, and curry leaves.
Pour tadka over mini dhoklas and serve.
notes: Mini size makes them perfect party snacks. Easy to pick up and eat.

name: Khaman Snack
preparation: Mix besan with oil, sugar, lemon juice, turmeric, salt, and water to make batter.
Add eno and mix gently.
Pour into greased plate and steam for 12-15 minutes.
Prepare tadka with mustard seeds, sesame seeds, and green chillies.
Pour tadka over khaman, garnish with coriander and coconut.
notes: Khaman is softer and yellower than dhokla. The tempering is key to its flavor.

name: Handvo Snack
preparation: Soak rice and dals for 4-5 hours. Grind to a coarse batter.
Let ferment overnight.
Add grated vegetables, turmeric, and salt to batter.
Heat oil in a pan with mustard seeds and sesame seeds.
Pour batter, cover and cook on low heat until golden on both sides.
notes: A Gujarati savory cake. Fermentation makes it nutritious and easy to digest.

name: Khandvi Snack
preparation: Mix besan, buttermilk, water, turmeric, and salt. Stir until smooth.
Cook on medium heat, stirring continuously for 10-12 minutes until thick.
Quickly spread thin layers on the back of greased plates.
Let cool slightly and roll tightly.
Cut into pieces, temper with mustard seeds, sesame, curry leaves. Garnish with coconut.
notes: Requires skill to spread thin. Work quickly before the mixture sets. A Gujarati delicacy.

name: Baked Samosa
preparation: Make firm dough with flour, 1 tbsp oil, salt, and water. Rest 20 minutes.
For filling: heat oil, add cumin seeds, mashed potatoes, peas, spices.
Roll dough, cut into semicircles, shape into cones.
Fill with potato mixture and seal edges.
Brush with oil and bake at 190°C for 25-30 minutes until golden.
notes: Baking makes these much lighter than deep-fried samosas. Serve with green chutney.

name: Mini Samosa Baked
preparation: Make stiff dough with maida, oil, salt, and water. Rest 15 minutes.
Prepare spiced potato-pea filling.
Roll small circles, cut in half, form mini cones.
Fill and seal edges with water.
Bake at 190°C for 20 minutes until crispy.
notes: Party-size samosas. Perfect for gatherings and kids' lunchboxes.

name: Baked Aloo Samosa
preparation: Prepare dough and rest for 20 minutes.
Mash potatoes, season with all spices and coriander.
Roll dough, shape into cones, fill with potato mixture.
Seal edges firmly.
Brush with oil and bake at 190°C for 25-30 minutes.
notes: Classic aloo filling baked to perfection. The amchur adds a tangy kick.

name: Spinach Samosa Baked
preparation: Make dough with flour, oil, salt, and water.
Blanch spinach, squeeze water, and chop finely.
Mix spinach with mashed potato, spices, and chilli.
Shape samosas and fill with spinach mixture.
Bake at 190°C for 25 minutes until golden.
notes: Spinach adds iron and makes the filling vibrant green. Healthier than regular samosas.

name: Aloo Tikki Snack
preparation: Mash potatoes and mix with cornflour, spices, and salt.
Shape into round flat tikkis.
Heat oil on a non-stick tawa.
Cook tikkis on medium heat until golden brown on both sides.
Serve hot with green chutney and tamarind chutney.
notes: Cornflour helps bind and gives a crispy exterior. A classic Delhi street food.

name: Corn Tikki Snack
preparation: Blend half the corn to a paste, keep half whole.
Mix corn paste, whole corn, mashed potato, besan, and spices.
Shape into round tikkis.
Pan-fry in oil until golden on both sides.
Serve hot with green chutney.
notes: Sweet corn gives a lovely sweetness. Partially blending creates the right texture.

name: Beetroot Tikki Snack
preparation: Grate boiled beetroot and squeeze out excess water.
Mix with mashed potato, besan, and spices.
Form into flat tikkis.
Pan-fry in oil until crispy on both sides.
Serve hot with yogurt dip.
notes: Beautiful pink tikkis rich in iron. Squeezing water is important for crispiness.

name: Sweet Potato Tikki Snack
preparation: Mash boiled sweet potatoes.
Add besan, cumin, chilli powder, chaat masala, and salt.
Mix well and shape into tikkis.
Pan-fry in oil until golden on both sides.
Serve hot with green chutney.
notes: Sweet potatoes have a lower glycemic index than regular potatoes. Naturally sweet and filling.

name: Papdi Chaat Light
preparation: Arrange papdis on a plate.
Top each with boiled chickpeas.
Drizzle whisked yogurt over them.
Add tamarind and green chutney.
Sprinkle chaat masala, cumin, sev, and coriander.
notes: Assemble just before serving to keep papdis crispy. A beloved chaat classic.

name: Bhel Puri Light
preparation: Place puffed rice in a large bowl.
Add chopped onion and tomato.
Add tamarind and green chutneys.
Sprinkle chaat masala and lemon juice.
Toss well, add sev and coriander. Serve immediately.
notes: Mumbai's iconic street snack. Must be eaten immediately or it gets soggy.

name: Sev Puri Light
preparation: Arrange flat puris on a plate.
Top each with diced potato and onion.
Add a dollop of tamarind and green chutney on each.
Sprinkle chaat masala.
Top generously with sev and coriander. Serve immediately.
notes: Each puri is a perfect one-bite explosion of flavors and textures.

name: Dahi Puri Light
preparation: Crack open the top of each golgappa shell.
Fill with a little potato and sprouts.
Spoon sweet yogurt generously into each.
Drizzle tamarind chutney.
Top with sev and sprinkle chaat masala and cumin.
notes: Sweet yogurt is the star. Can use hung curd for thicker consistency.

name: Corn Chaat
preparation: Boil or steam corn kernels until tender.
Heat butter in a pan, add corn and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Sprinkle chaat masala and lemon juice.
Garnish with coriander and serve warm.
notes: A quick, healthy chaat. Fresh corn on the cob tastes best when in season.

name: Masala Corn Cup
preparation: Boil corn kernels until tender. Drain.
Heat butter in a pan.
Add corn, chilli powder, chaat masala, and salt.
Toss well for 1-2 minutes.
Squeeze lemon juice, garnish with coriander. Serve in cups.
notes: A popular street-side snack served in paper cups. Quick and satisfying.

name: Corn Bhutta
preparation: Grill corn directly on flame or on grill, rotating frequently.
Grill for 8-10 minutes until lightly charred all around.
Rub lemon half dipped in salt and chilli powder all over the corn.
Sprinkle chaat masala.
Serve hot.
notes: A monsoon and winter street food classic. The charring adds smoky flavor.

name: Sweet Corn Salad
preparation: Boil corn and let cool.
Mix corn with cucumber, bell pepper, and onion.
Add lemon juice, chaat masala, salt, and pepper.
Toss well.
Garnish with coriander.
notes: A light and refreshing snack. Bell peppers add color and vitamin C.

name: Fruit Chaat
preparation: Wash and cut all fruits into bite-size pieces.
Place in a large bowl.
Add chaat masala, black salt, lemon juice, and chilli powder.
Toss gently to combine.
Serve immediately.
notes: Use any seasonal fruits. The masala transforms fruit into an exciting Indian snack.

name: Masala Fruit Bowl
preparation: Cut all fruits into uniform cubes.
Arrange in a bowl.
Sprinkle chaat masala and black salt.
Squeeze lemon juice over the top.
Garnish with mint leaves and serve.
notes: A vitamin-rich, low-calorie snack. Great for hot summer afternoons.

name: Spicy Fruit Salad
preparation: Cut all fruits into pieces.
Mix in a large bowl.
Add red chilli, chaat masala, black salt, and lemon.
Toss well.
Serve immediately.
notes: Raw mango adds a wonderful tanginess. Adjust chilli based on spice preference.

name: Chaat Masala Fruit
ingredients: 1 cup mixed fruits (apple, grapes, banana, orange)
preparation: Wash and cut all fruits into bite-size pieces.
Place in a serving bowl.
Sprinkle chaat masala, black salt, and pepper.
Drizzle lemon juice.
Toss gently and serve.
notes: The simplest fruit snack. Black pepper adds a warming kick.

name: Murmura Chivda
preparation: Dry roast puffed rice until crisp. Set aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add peanuts and roast for a minute.
Add turmeric and chilli powder.
Add puffed rice, salt, toss well. Cool and store.
notes: A Maharashtrian staple. Can be stored in airtight container for a week.

name: Poha Chivda
preparation: Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add peanuts and raisins, fry until raisins puff.
Add turmeric and thick poha.
Roast on low heat for 10-12 minutes, stirring often.
Add salt, sugar, and chilli powder. Cool completely.
notes: Use thick poha for chivda, not thin. Stays crispy for weeks when stored properly.

name: Oats Chivda
preparation: Dry roast oats until light and crispy. Set aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add peanuts and raisins.
Add oats back to pan with turmeric, salt, and sugar.
Mix well, cool completely and store.
notes: A healthy twist on traditional chivda. Oats add fiber and keep you full.

name: Rice Flakes Chivda
preparation: Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add peanuts and cashews, fry until golden.
Add beaten rice flakes and roast on low for 10 minutes.
Add turmeric, chilli powder, salt, and a pinch of sugar.
Cool completely and store in airtight jar.
notes: A Diwali special snack. The mix of peanuts and cashews adds great texture.

name: Roasted Peanuts
preparation: Dry roast peanuts in a heavy pan on medium heat for 8-10 minutes.
Stir frequently to avoid burning.
They should become crunchy and skins will darken.
Remove from heat, sprinkle salt, chilli, and chaat masala.
Toss and let cool.
notes: Rich in protein and healthy fats. A simple, satisfying snack.

name: Masala Peanuts
preparation: Mix besan, rice flour, chilli, turmeric, ajwain, and salt.
Add just enough water to make a thick coating.
Coat peanuts evenly with the batter.
Spread on baking tray and bake at 180°C for 20-25 minutes.
Toss midway for even roasting. Cool completely.
notes: Baked version is healthier than the deep-fried original. Ajwain aids digestion.

name: Boiled Peanut Chaat
preparation: Boil peanuts in salted water for 20-25 minutes until tender.
Drain and let cool slightly.
Mix with onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Add chaat masala, lemon juice, and salt.
Garnish with coriander.
notes: A monsoon favorite. Boiled peanuts are softer and easier to digest than roasted.

name: Peanut Chikki Light
preparation: Melt jaggery in a pan on low heat until it forms a smooth syrup.
Test by dropping in water - it should form a hard ball.
Add roasted peanuts and cardamom quickly.
Mix well and pour onto greased plate.
Flatten with a rolling pin, cool, and break into pieces.
notes: Jaggery must reach the right temperature. A traditional winter energy snack.

name: Khakhra
preparation: Mix flour, oil, turmeric, chilli, and salt.
Knead into stiff dough with water.
Divide into small balls and roll thin like chapati.
Roast on hot tawa, pressing with cloth until crispy on both sides.
Should become thin and crunchy. Cool and store.
notes: A staple Gujarati snack. Roll as thin as possible for maximum crispiness.

name: Masala Khakhra
preparation: Combine flour with all spices, oil, and salt.
Knead firm dough with minimal water.
Roll very thin circles.
Cook on hot tawa, pressing continuously until crispy.
Both sides should be evenly cooked and crunchy.
notes: The spice blend gives a more flavorful version. Great with tea.

name: Methi Khakhra
preparation: Mix flour, methi leaves, salt, turmeric, and ajwain.
Add oil and knead into a stiff dough with minimal water.
Divide into small balls and roll very thin.
Roast on a hot tawa pressing with a cloth until crispy on both sides.
Cool completely before storing.
notes: Methi adds a slightly bitter, earthy flavor. Great with pickle or tea.

name: Bajra Khakhra
preparation: Mix bajra flour, wheat flour, salt, cumin, and chilli powder.
Add oil and knead with warm water into a smooth dough.
Roll into thin circles between plastic sheets.
Roast on tawa pressing down until crispy on both sides.
Cool and store in airtight container.
notes: Bajra is rich in iron and great for winters. The plastic sheet trick prevents sticking.

name: Mathri Baked
preparation: Mix flour, salt, ajwain, and pepper.
Rub in ghee until mixture resembles breadcrumbs.
Knead into a stiff dough with minimal water.
Roll out thick and cut into small rounds. Prick with a fork.
Bake at 180°C for 20-25 minutes until golden and crispy.
notes: Baking instead of frying cuts calories significantly. Pricking prevents puffing.

name: Namak Para Baked
preparation: Combine flour, salt, ajwain, pepper, and cumin.
Add oil and rub into the flour.
Knead into a firm dough with water.
Roll out and cut into diamond shapes.
Bake at 180°C for 18-20 minutes until crispy and golden.
notes: These baked namak para are much lighter than the traditional fried version.

name: Shakkar Para Light
preparation: Mix flour, jaggery, cardamom, and salt.
Rub in ghee and knead into a stiff dough.
Roll out thick and cut into diamond shapes.
Bake at 170°C for 20-22 minutes until golden.
Cool completely - they crisp up as they cool.
notes: A healthier baked version of the traditional sweet snack. Jaggery adds minerals.

name: Mini Matthi
preparation: Mix flour, salt, pepper, and ajwain.
Rub ghee into the flour until crumbly.
Knead a stiff dough using water.
Roll small balls, flatten into mini discs. Prick with fork.
Bake at 180°C for 18-20 minutes until crisp.
notes: Mini size makes portion control easier. Perfect with masala chai.

name: Paneer Tikka Snack
preparation: Mix yogurt, tikka masala, turmeric, chilli, and ginger-garlic paste.
Marinate paneer cubes for 30 minutes.
Thread paneer, capsicum, and onion on skewers.
Brush with oil and grill or bake at 200°C for 15 minutes.
Serve hot with mint chutney.
notes: Press paneer before marinating for better texture and absorption.

name: Paneer Cubes Masala
preparation: Cut paneer into bite-sized cubes.
Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds.
Add paneer cubes and sauté until lightly golden.
Sprinkle chaat masala, chilli powder, and black salt.
Squeeze lemon and garnish with coriander.
notes: Quick 5-minute snack. Use fresh paneer for the best taste.

name: Grilled Paneer Snack
preparation: Slice paneer into thick rectangles.
Mix oil, garlic powder, herbs, pepper, salt, and lemon juice.
Brush paneer slices with the mixture.
Grill on a hot grill pan for 2-3 minutes each side.
Serve with green chutney or ketchup.
notes: Get nice grill marks by not moving the paneer too soon. High protein snack.

name: Moong Dal Pakora Baked
preparation: Drain soaked moong dal and grind to a coarse paste with chilli and ginger.
Add hing, salt, and cumin seeds to the batter.
Drop small spoonfuls onto a greased baking tray.
Brush tops with oil.
Bake at 200°C for 20-25 minutes until golden and crispy.
notes: Baking makes them oil-free and guilt-free. The dal doesn't need to be soaked too long.

name: Palak Pakora Baked
preparation: Mix besan, rice flour, salt, chilli powder, and ajwain.
Add very little water to make a thick coating batter.
Dip each spinach leaf in batter.
Place on a greased baking tray and brush with oil.
Bake at 200°C for 15-18 minutes until crispy.
notes: Use large flat spinach leaves for best results. Great monsoon snack.

name: Onion Pakora Baked
preparation: Mix sliced onions with salt and let sit 10 minutes to release moisture.
Add besan, rice flour, chilli powder, turmeric, and green chilli.
Mix well without adding water - onion moisture is enough.
Shape into small flat rounds on a greased tray.
Bake at 200°C for 20 minutes, flip and bake 10 more minutes.
notes: The onion moisture trick avoids adding extra water. Crispy edges guaranteed.

name: Bread Pakora Baked
preparation: Mix mashed potato with chaat masala, amchur, and green chilli.
Cut bread slices in triangles and spread potato filling.
Sandwich two triangles together.
Dip in besan batter and place on greased baking tray.
Brush with oil and bake at 190°C for 18-20 minutes until golden.
notes: Use day-old bread for better texture. Serve with tamarind and green chutney.

name: Sabudana Chivda
preparation: Soak sabudana for 1 hour, drain and dry completely.
Heat oil and fry sabudana in batches until puffed and crispy.
In same oil, add curry leaves and green chillies.
Toss fried sabudana with peanuts, sugar, and salt.
Cool completely before storing.
notes: Popular during Navratri fasting. Ensure sabudana is completely dry before frying.

name: Sabudana Cutlet Baked
preparation: Mix soaked sabudana, mashed potato, peanuts, chilli, cumin, and salt.
Shape into flat round cutlets.
Place on a greased baking tray.
Brush with oil on both sides.
Bake at 190°C for 20 minutes, flip and bake 10 more minutes until crispy.
notes: Fasting-friendly snack. The peanuts add crunch and protein.

name: Sabudana Vada Snack
preparation: Mix sabudana, potato, peanuts, chillies, coriander, and salt.
Shape into thick round patties.
Heat oil in a non-stick pan.
Shallow fry the vadas until golden on both sides using minimal oil.
Drain on paper towel and serve with green chutney.
notes: Classic Maharashtrian fasting snack. Drain sabudana well for crispy vadas.

name: Idli Fry Snack
preparation: Cut idlis into bite-sized cubes.
Heat oil and add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add idli cubes and sauté on medium heat.
Sprinkle chilli powder, turmeric, and salt.
Toss until idli cubes are crispy on the outside.
notes: Best way to use leftover idlis. Day-old idlis fry better than fresh ones.

name: Mini Idli Snack
preparation: Steam mini idlis in a mini idli mold.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, curry leaves, and red chillies.
Add steamed mini idlis to the tempering.
Toss gently to coat evenly.
Garnish with grated coconut and serve.
notes: Also called button idli. Popular South Indian party snack.

name: Idli Manchurian Snack
preparation: Cut idlis into cubes and pan-fry until slightly crispy.
Heat oil and sauté onion and capsicum.
Add soy sauce, ketchup, and chilli sauce.
Add cornflour slurry and mix well.
Toss in fried idli cubes and coat evenly with the sauce.
notes: Indo-Chinese twist on leftover idlis. Adjust sauces to your spice preference.

name: Dosa Rolls Snack
preparation: Prepare thin dosas using the batter on a hot tawa.
Make quick potato filling with mustard seeds, turmeric, chilli, and salt.
Place a spoonful of potato filling on the dosa.
Roll tightly and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Serve with coconut chutney.
notes: Great party finger food. Can make ahead and reheat in oven.

name: Mini Dosa Snack
preparation: Heat a tawa and grease lightly.
Pour small spoonfuls of batter to make 3-inch mini dosas.
Cook until the edges are crispy and golden.
Flip and cook for another minute.
Serve with coconut and tomato chutney.
notes: Fun bite-sized version perfect for snacking. Kids love these.

name: Dosa Fingers
preparation: Spread dosa batter in a thin rectangle on hot tawa.
Cook until crispy on the bottom.
Flip and cook briefly.
Cut into finger-sized strips while still on the tawa.
Sprinkle chaat masala and serve with chutney.
notes: Extra crispy dosa strips. Make the dosa thinner for maximum crunch.

name: Roasted Foxnuts Pepper
preparation: Heat ghee in a pan on low flame.
Add makhana and dry roast for 8-10 minutes, stirring continuously.
Sprinkle black pepper, salt, and chaat masala.
Toss well to coat evenly.
Cool completely for maximum crunch.
notes: Black pepper adds warmth. Low calorie, high volume snack perfect for weight loss.

name: Roasted Cashews
preparation: Heat ghee in a pan on low flame.
Add cashews and roast on low heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring.
Add salt, pepper, and turmeric.
Toss well and continue roasting for 2 more minutes.
Cool on a plate - they crisp up as they cool.
notes: Calorie-dense, so stick to a small handful. Good source of magnesium.

name: Mixed Dry Fruit Snack
preparation: Lightly roast almonds, cashews, and walnuts separately in a dry pan.
Cool completely.
Mix all roasted nuts with raisins and cranberries.
Add a tiny pinch of salt.
Store in an airtight jar.
notes: Keep a jar ready for a quick healthy grab. Rich in omega-3 and vitamin E.

name: Murmura Namkeen
preparation: Dry roast murmura in a pan until crispy, set aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Add turmeric, chilli powder, and peanuts.
Add roasted murmura, salt, and sugar.
Toss well and cool completely.
notes: The most common Indian tea-time snack. Quick to make and very light.

name: Kurmura Bhel
preparation: Put puffed rice in a large bowl.
Add onion, tomato, and boiled potato.
Add tamarind and green chutney.
Sprinkle chaat masala and squeeze lemon.
Toss well, top with sev, and serve immediately.
notes: Must be served immediately or puffed rice gets soggy. Street food classic.

name: Churmuri
ingredients: 1/2 raw mango or 1 tbsp lemon juice
preparation: Mix puffed rice with onion and raw mango or lemon juice.
Add peanuts, green chilli, and coconut.
Sprinkle salt and toss well.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve immediately in paper cones for authentic experience.
notes: Karnataka's version of bhel puri. The coconut makes it distinct.

name: Roasted Channa Dal
preparation: Soak channa dal for 2 hours, drain and dry thoroughly.
Spread on a baking tray.
Bake at 170°C for 25-30 minutes until golden and crunchy.
Sprinkle salt, chilli powder, chaat masala, and turmeric.
Toss and cool completely.
notes: High protein crunchy snack. Also called dalia. Great for munching.

name: Roasted Moong Dal
preparation: Wash and soak moong dal for 1 hour, drain and pat dry.
Spread on a baking tray.
Bake at 170°C for 20-25 minutes until crunchy.
Mix salt, turmeric, chilli powder, and amchur.
Toss the roasted dal with the spice mix.
notes: Lighter and easier to digest than channa dal. Protein-rich snack.

name: Dal Moth Light
preparation: Soak dals separately, drain and dry well.
Fry in minimal oil in batches until crispy.
Roast peanuts in the same pan.
Mix all fried ingredients with raisins.
Season with salt, chilli, chaat masala, and amchur.
notes: Classic Delhi snack mix. Use an air fryer for even healthier version.

name: Veggie Sticks Chutney
preparation: Wash and cut all vegetables into even-sized sticks.
Keep them in ice water for 10 minutes for extra crunch.
Drain and pat dry.
Arrange on a plate with chutneys in the center.
Serve chilled.
notes: The lowest calorie snack option. Ice water soak makes veggies extra crispy.

name: Cucumber Sticks Chutney
preparation: Peel and cut cucumbers into thick sticks.
Sprinkle chaat masala and black salt.
Squeeze lemon juice over them.
Serve with mint chutney on the side.
Keep chilled until serving.
notes: Almost zero-calorie snack. Hydrating and refreshing for summers.

name: Carrot Sticks Hummus Indian
preparation: Blend boiled chana, tahini, lemon juice, garlic, cumin, and salt into smooth hummus.
Drizzle olive oil on top.
Cut carrots into even sticks.
Arrange carrot sticks around the hummus bowl.
Serve chilled.
notes: Indian-style hummus using chana instead of chickpeas. Til paste works as tahini substitute.

name: Ragi Cookies
preparation: Cream butter and jaggery together.
Add ragi flour, cardamom, and baking soda.
Add milk gradually to form a soft dough.
Shape into small cookies and place on a lined baking tray.
Bake at 170°C for 15-18 minutes. Cool on rack.
notes: Ragi is rich in calcium and iron. These cookies are naturally gluten-friendly.

name: Oats Cookies Indian
preparation: Mix oats, wheat flour, jaggery, cardamom, and baking soda.
Add melted ghee and mix well.
Add milk to bring dough together.
Shape into flat cookies and top with chopped almonds.
Bake at 170°C for 18-20 minutes until golden.
notes: Indian-style oats cookies using jaggery instead of sugar. Fiber-rich snack.

name: Nachni Biscuits
preparation: Cream butter and sugar together.
Add nachni flour, cardamom, and salt.
Add milk and form a smooth dough.
Roll out and cut with cookie cutters.
Bake at 165°C for 15-17 minutes. Cool on wire rack.
notes: Nachni is the same as ragi/finger millet. Great for growing kids.

name: Wheat Rusk Healthy
preparation: Cut bread slices into halves or thirds.
Brush lightly with ghee.
Sprinkle cardamom and jaggery powder.
Arrange on baking tray.
Bake at 150°C for 25-30 minutes until completely dry and crispy.
notes: Homemade rusks are much healthier than store-bought. Perfect for dipping in chai.

name: Suji Toast
preparation: Mix suji, yogurt, chilli, coriander, and salt. Let sit 10 minutes.
Spread the suji mixture thickly on bread slices.
Heat oil in a pan.
Place bread suji-side down and cook until golden.
Flip and toast the other side. Cut into triangles.
notes: Let the suji batter rest so it swells and creates a fluffy topping.

name: Bread Toast Masala
preparation: Apply thin layer of butter on both bread slices.
Mix chaat masala, chilli powder, oregano, and salt.
Sprinkle the spice mix evenly on the bread.
Toast on a tawa until crispy and golden on both sides.
Cut into triangles and serve hot.
notes: Simplest snack when you have nothing else. Ready in 3 minutes.

name: Egg Sandwich Light
preparation: Boil eggs, peel and chop finely.
Mix with mayo, mustard, salt, pepper, and chaat masala.
Place lettuce on one bread slice.
Spread egg mixture over lettuce.
Top with second slice and cut diagonally.
notes: High-protein snack. Use whole boiled eggs for maximum nutrition.

name: Paneer Sandwich Light
preparation: Crumble paneer and mix with capsicum, onion, salt, pepper, and chaat masala.
Spread green chutney on both bread slices.
Layer the paneer mixture on one slice.
Press the second slice on top.
Grill on a tawa or sandwich maker until golden.
notes: Fresh paneer crumbles have a lovely texture. Press sandwich gently while grilling.

name: Veg Sandwich Grilled
preparation: Spread green chutney on both bread slices.
Layer cucumber, tomato, onion, capsicum, and potato slices.
Sprinkle chaat masala.
Close the sandwich and butter the outside.
Grill on a hot tawa or sandwich press until golden.
notes: Mumbai-style Bombay sandwich. The green chutney is the star - make it fresh.

name: Momos Mini Snack
preparation: Knead maida with water and salt into a smooth dough. Rest 20 minutes.
Sauté vegetables with soy sauce, salt, and pepper for filling.
Roll small circles, place filling, and fold into mini momos.
Steam in a steamer for 10-12 minutes.
Serve hot with spicy red chutney.
notes: Mini size makes them perfect as a snack rather than a meal.

name: Steamed Veg Momos
preparation: Make smooth dough with maida, water, and pinch of salt. Rest covered.
Mix chopped veggies with spring onion, ginger, soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, and pepper.
Roll thin circles, fill, and pleat into momo shape.
Steam for 12-15 minutes until wrapper is translucent.
Serve with fiery red momos chutney.
notes: The sesame oil in the filling adds authentic flavor. Don't overstuff.

name: Pan Fried Momos Light
preparation: Knead dough with maida, water, and salt. Rest 20 minutes.
Mix paneer, cabbage, soy sauce, ginger-garlic paste, salt, and pepper.
Fill and shape momos.
Steam for 10 minutes first.
Then pan-fry in a little oil until bottoms are golden and crispy.
notes: Steam first, then fry for the best texture - crispy bottom, soft top.

name: Spring Roll Mini
preparation: Stir-fry vegetables with soy sauce, vinegar, salt, and pepper. Cool.
Cut spring roll sheets into halves for mini size.
Place a spoonful of filling and roll tightly, sealing with maida paste.
Brush with oil and bake at 200°C for 15 minutes.
Serve with sweet chilli sauce.
notes: Baking makes them lighter. Seal edges properly to prevent filling from leaking.

name: Veg Spring Roll Baked
preparation: Stir-fry julienned vegetables with soy sauce, sesame oil, ginger.
Season with salt and pepper. Cool the filling.
Place filling on wrapper, fold sides in, and roll tightly.
Place on a baking tray and brush with oil.
Bake at 200°C for 18-20 minutes until golden and crispy.
notes: Turn rolls halfway through baking for even browning.

name: Paneer Spring Roll
preparation: Sauté capsicum and cabbage briefly.
Add crumbled paneer, soy sauce, chilli sauce, and salt. Cool.
Place filling on wrappers and roll tightly.
Seal edges with maida paste.
Brush with oil and bake at 200°C for 15-18 minutes.
notes: Paneer adds protein to the classic spring roll. Don't overcrumble the paneer.

name: Lassi Sweet
preparation: Blend yogurt, sugar, water, and cardamom until smooth and frothy.
Add ice cubes and blend briefly.
Pour into a tall glass.
Garnish with chopped pistachios.
Serve immediately chilled.
notes: Use full-fat yogurt for authentic Punjabi-style thickness. Adjust sweetness to taste.

name: Lassi Salt
preparation: Blend yogurt, water, salt, and cumin powder until frothy.
Add ice cubes and blend briefly.
Pour into a glass.
Sprinkle black salt and garnish with mint.
Serve chilled.
notes: Salt lassi is great for digestion and rehydration. Lower calorie than sweet lassi.

name: Chaas Masala
preparation: Whisk yogurt and water until smooth.
Add cumin powder, black salt, and regular salt.
Crush curry leaves slightly and add.
Add slit green chilli and coriander.
Serve chilled with ice.
notes: Traditional Gujarati buttermilk. The curry leaves add a wonderful aroma.

name: Buttermilk Spiced
preparation: Blend yogurt and water until smooth.
Add cumin, pepper, hing, and salt.
Mix well.
Garnish with fresh coriander.
Serve chilled or at room temperature.
notes: Ayurvedic digestive drink. Best consumed after meals for digestion.

name: Coconut Water Fresh
preparation: Choose a tender green coconut.
Cut the top off carefully.
Insert a straw.
Drink the water fresh.
Scoop out the soft malai (flesh) to eat.
notes: Nature's electrolyte drink. Best consumed fresh from tender coconuts.

name: Nimbu Paani
preparation: Squeeze fresh lemon juice into a glass.
Add cold water, sugar, black salt, and cumin powder.
Stir well until sugar dissolves.
Add ice cubes.
Garnish with mint leaves and serve.
notes: India's most popular summer drink. Black salt is the secret ingredient.

name: Aam Panna
preparation: Boil or roast raw mango until soft. Peel and extract pulp.
Blend pulp with jaggery, cumin, black salt, and pepper.
Add cold water and mix well.
Strain if desired.
Serve with ice and garnish with mint.
notes: Excellent for preventing heat stroke. Raw mango is rich in vitamin C.

name: Jaljeera
ingredients: 2 tbsp jaljeera powder or 1 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp black pepper, 1/4 tsp hing, dry mint
preparation: If making from scratch, dry roast and grind cumin, pepper, hing, and dry mint.
Mix jaljeera powder with cold water.
Add lemon juice and black salt.
Stir well and add ice cubes.
Garnish with fresh mint and coriander.
notes: Classic Indian appetizer drink. Nearly zero calories and great for digestion.

name: Chana Jor Garam Cup
preparation: Put chana jor in a bowl.
Add onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Sprinkle chaat masala and squeeze lemon.
Toss everything together.
Top with sev and coriander. Serve in paper cups.
notes: Classic street food snack. The flattened chana gives it a unique texture.

name: Kurkure Baked
preparation: Mix besan, rice flour, cornmeal, and all spices.
Add water gradually to make a stiff dough.
Fill in a sev maker or piping bag and press strips onto a greased tray.
Brush with oil.
Bake at 180°C for 15-18 minutes until crispy.
notes: Homemade healthy version of the popular snack. Adjust spice level to preference.

name: Masala Papad
preparation: Roast or microwave papads until crispy.
Mix onion, tomato, chilli, coriander, chaat masala, and lemon juice.
Spread the topping evenly over crispy papads.
Break into pieces or serve whole.
Serve immediately before papads soften.
notes: Quick no-cook snack. Roasting papad on direct flame gives the best flavor.

name: Roasted Sweet Potato
preparation: Wash sweet potato and prick with a fork.
Roast directly on gas flame or bake at 200°C for 40 minutes.
Peel the charred skin.
Cut into chunks and place in a bowl.
Season with chaat masala, chilli, black salt, and lemon juice.
notes: Sweet potato is rich in beta-carotene and fiber. Roasting on flame adds a smoky flavor.

name: Roasted Corn On Cob
preparation: Roast corn directly on gas flame, turning frequently.
Roast until evenly charred with some blackened kernels.
Rub butter all over the hot corn.
Sprinkle salt and chilli powder.
Squeeze lemon generously and enjoy hot.
notes: Monsoon street food favorite. The lemon-butter-chilli combo is iconic.

name: Tandoori Soya Chunks
preparation: Boil soya chunks for 5 minutes, drain and squeeze out water.
Mix yogurt, tandoori masala, chilli, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, and salt.
Marinate soya chunks for 30 minutes.
Thread on skewers and brush with oil.
Grill or bake at 200°C for 15 minutes, turning once.
notes: Soya chunks are 52% protein. Squeeze out all water after boiling for better marinade absorption.

name: Baked Falafel Indian
preparation: Drain soaked chana and grind coarsely with coriander and chillies.
Add cumin, coriander powder, garam masala, and salt.
Shape into small flat patties.
Place on greased baking tray and brush with oil.
Bake at 190°C for 25 minutes, flipping halfway.
notes: Indian-spiced falafel with garam masala twist. Serve with mint chutney instead of tahini.

name: Hummus Indian Style
preparation: Blend boiled chana with til paste, lemon juice, garlic, and cumin.
Add salt and blend until creamy smooth.
Transfer to a bowl and make a swirl.
Drizzle olive oil and sprinkle chaat masala and paprika.
Serve with khakhra or veggie sticks.
notes: Using til (sesame) paste as a tahini substitute makes it more Indian.

name: Mint Chutney Dip
preparation: Wash mint and coriander thoroughly.
Blend all ingredients with water until smooth.
Adjust salt and lemon to taste.
Transfer to a serving bowl.
Serve as a dip with snacks, sandwiches, or veggie sticks.
notes: Stays fresh for 3 days refrigerated. Add a pinch of sugar if too tangy.

name: Tamarind Chutney Dip
preparation: Soak tamarind in warm water and extract pulp.
Cook tamarind pulp with jaggery and water on low heat.
Add cumin, chilli powder, black salt, and salt.
Simmer for 10 minutes until thickened.
Strain and cool. Store in a jar.
notes: Essential accompaniment for chaat. Can be stored refrigerated for 2 weeks.

name: Green Chutney Dip
preparation: Wash coriander thoroughly.
Blend coriander, chillies, chana dal, and ginger.
Add lemon juice, sugar, and salt.
Add water to adjust consistency.
Blend until smooth and serve.
notes: The roasted chana dal adds body and keeps the chutney from becoming too watery.

name: Coconut Chutney Dip
ingredients: tempering: 1 tsp oil, mustard seeds, curry leaves, dried red chilli
preparation: Blend coconut, chillies, ginger, chana dal, coriander, and salt with water.
Heat oil for tempering, add mustard seeds.
When they splutter, add curry leaves and red chilli.
Pour tempering over the chutney.
Mix and serve with dosa, idli, or as a dip.
notes: South Indian staple dip. Fresh coconut is key - avoid desiccated.

name: Mixed Nuts Roasted
preparation: Heat ghee in a pan on low flame.
Add all nuts and roast on low heat for 8-10 minutes.
Add pumpkin seeds in the last 2 minutes.
Sprinkle salt, pepper, and chaat masala.
Cool completely and store airtight.
notes: Healthy fat-rich snack. Keep portions small as nuts are calorie dense.

name: Trail Mix Indian
preparation: Roast chana, peanuts, and makhana separately if not already roasted.
Cool all roasted ingredients.
Mix everything together in a large bowl.
Add chaat masala and salt. Toss well.
Store in airtight containers for up to 2 weeks.
notes: Indian-style trail mix with desi ingredients. Perfect portable snack.

name: Seeds Mix Indian
preparation: Dry roast each type of seed separately on low heat.
Cool all seeds.
Mix together in a bowl.
Season with salt and chaat masala.
Store in an airtight jar.
notes: Superfood mix rich in omega-3 and zinc. Sprinkle on salads or eat as is.

name: Flax Seed Crackers
preparation: Soak flax seeds in 1/4 cup water for 15 minutes until gel forms.
Mix in flour, salt, cumin, and ajwain.
Roll out very thin between parchment sheets.
Cut into squares or crackers.
Bake at 170°C for 20-25 minutes until crispy.
notes: Rich in omega-3 fatty acids. The flax gel acts as a natural binder.

name: Ragi Crackers
preparation: Mix ragi flour, rice flour, salt, cumin, and sesame seeds.
Add oil and enough water to make a stiff dough.
Roll out very thin on parchment paper.
Score into rectangles with a knife.
Bake at 170°C for 18-20 minutes until crispy.
notes: Ragi is rich in calcium. These crackers are naturally gluten-free.

name: Multigrain Crackers
preparation: Mix all flours, salt, ajwain, and sesame seeds.
Add oil and knead with water into a stiff dough.
Roll out paper-thin between plastic sheets.
Cut into desired shapes.
Bake at 170°C for 15-18 minutes until crispy.
notes: Four grain power in every cracker. Great alternative to processed snacks.

name: Jaggery Peanut Balls
preparation: Dry roast peanuts and crush coarsely.
Heat jaggery with ghee until it melts and forms a sticky syrup.
Add cardamom powder.
Mix in crushed peanuts quickly.
Shape into small balls while still warm.
notes: Traditional winter snack. Work quickly as mixture hardens fast. Rich in iron from jaggery.

name: Date Walnut Balls
preparation: Soak dates in warm water for 10 minutes if hard.
Blend dates and walnuts into a sticky paste.
Add cardamom and cocoa powder if using.
Roll into small balls.
Coat with desiccated coconut.
notes: No added sugar - dates provide all the sweetness. Natural energy booster.

name: Sesame Ladoo Light
preparation: Dry roast sesame seeds on low heat until golden. Be careful not to burn.
Melt jaggery with ghee on low heat until syrupy.
Add cardamom powder.
Mix in roasted sesame seeds.
Shape into small ladoos while warm.
notes: Til ladoo is a Makar Sankranti specialty. Rich in calcium from sesame seeds.

name: Roasted Almonds Indian
preparation: Heat ghee in a pan on low flame.
Add almonds and roast on low heat for 7-8 minutes.
Add salt, turmeric, and chilli powder.
Toss well and roast for 2 more minutes.
Cool completely on a plate.
notes: Soaking almonds overnight and then roasting them makes them easier to digest.

name: Spiced Cashew Snack
preparation: Heat oil and fry curry leaves until crispy.
Add cashews and roast on low heat for 5-6 minutes.
Add turmeric, chilli powder, salt, and sugar.
Toss well and roast 2 more minutes.
Cool completely - they crisp up more as they cool.
notes: South Indian style spiced cashews. The curry leaves add incredible flavor.

name: Masala Trail Mix
preparation: Ensure all nuts and chana are well roasted and cooled.
Mix all dry ingredients in a large bowl.
Add chaat masala, chilli powder, and salt.
Toss well to coat evenly.
Store in an airtight container.
notes: Keep a jar at your desk for healthy munching. Customise with your favorite nuts.

name: Sattu Balls
preparation: Mix sattu, jaggery, and cardamom powder.
Add melted ghee and mix until it comes together.
Add chopped almonds.
Shape into small round balls.
Store in an airtight container.
notes: Sattu is a Bihar specialty - roasted gram flour that's a natural protein powerhouse.

name: Sattu Ladoo
preparation: Mix sattu and jaggery powder evenly.
Add melted ghee and cardamom.
Knead into a dough-like consistency.
Roll into round ladoos.
Roll each ladoo in desiccated coconut.
notes: High-energy snack popular in Bihar and UP. Perfect pre or post workout.

name: Chana Sattu Snack
preparation: Mix sattu with onion, green chilli, and coriander.
Add lemon juice, salt, and mustard oil.
Mix well - it should be slightly moist but not wet.
Shape into small portions or serve as is.
Enjoy as a savory protein-rich snack.
notes: Savory version of sattu. The mustard oil is essential for authentic taste.

name: Vegetable Cutlet Baked
ingredients: 1/4 cup mixed veggies (peas, carrot, beans), boiled and chopped
preparation: Mix mashed potatoes, vegetables, paneer, garam masala, amchur, and salt.
Shape into flat oval cutlets.
Roll in breadcrumbs for a crunchy coating.
Place on a greased tray and brush with oil.
Bake at 190°C for 20 minutes, flip and bake 10 more minutes.
notes: Baked cutlets are 60% less oily than fried. Paneer adds binding and protein.

name: Corn Cutlet Baked
preparation: Blend half the corn to a paste, keep half whole.
Mix corn paste, whole corn, potato, cornflour, chilli, cumin, and salt.
Shape into round cutlets.
Coat with breadcrumbs and place on greased tray.
Brush with oil and bake at 190°C for 25 minutes, flipping once.
notes: Half blended, half whole corn gives great texture. Kids love these.

name: Mixed Veg Cutlet
preparation: Mix mashed potatoes with all chopped vegetables.
Add garam masala and salt.
Shape into flat cutlets.
Coat with breadcrumbs.
Bake at 190°C for 25 minutes or shallow fry with minimal oil.
notes: Great way to sneak in vegetables. Adjust veggies based on what's available.

name: Soya Cutlet
ingredients: 1 cup soya granules, soaked and squeezed
preparation: Soak soya granules in hot water for 10 minutes, squeeze dry.
Mix with mashed potato, onion, chilli, garam masala, amchur, and salt.
Shape into flat cutlets.
Coat in breadcrumbs and place on greased tray.
Bake at 190°C for 25 minutes, turning once.
notes: High-protein cutlet. Squeeze all water from soya for better binding.

name: Rajma Cutlet
preparation: Mash boiled rajma coarsely with a fork.
Mix with potato, onion, chilli, cumin, garam masala, and salt.
Shape into flat cutlets.
Coat with breadcrumbs.
Bake at 190°C for 20-25 minutes or pan-fry with minimal oil.
notes: Rajma adds fiber and protein. Don't over-mash - keep some texture.

name: Paneer Cutlet Baked
preparation: Mix grated paneer, mashed potato, capsicum, chilli, garam masala, and salt.
Shape into flat round cutlets.
Coat each cutlet in breadcrumbs.
Place on greased baking tray and brush with oil.
Bake at 190°C for 20 minutes, turning halfway.
notes: Grating paneer instead of crumbling gives better binding. Protein-rich snack.

name: Aloo Bonda Baked
preparation: Make tempering with mustard seeds, curry leaves, and chilli in a little oil.
Mix with mashed potatoes, turmeric, and salt.
Shape into round balls.
Dip in besan batter and place on greased tray.
Bake at 200°C for 20-25 minutes until golden.
notes: South Indian style bonda, baked for a healthier version. Serve with coconut chutney.

name: Cheese Balls Baked
preparation: Mix potato, cheese, cornflour, pepper, oregano, and salt.
Refrigerate for 15 minutes.
Shape into small balls.
Roll in breadcrumbs and place on greased tray.
Bake at 200°C for 18-20 minutes until golden and crispy.
notes: Refrigerating helps them hold shape. Use processed cheese for better melt.

name: Veg Nuggets Baked
ingredients: 1 cup mixed veggies (carrot, peas, corn, beans), boiled and chopped
preparation: Mix boiled veggies, potato, bread crumbs, garam masala, and salt.
Shape into nugget shapes.
Coat in breadcrumbs.
Place on greased baking tray and brush with oil.
Bake at 190°C for 20-22 minutes, turning halfway.
notes: Healthy homemade nuggets for kids. Way better than store-bought frozen ones.

name: Banana Chips Baked
preparation: Slice raw bananas very thin using a mandoline.
Toss slices with coconut oil, turmeric, and salt.
Arrange in a single layer on baking trays.
Bake at 180°C for 15-18 minutes until crispy.
Cool completely - they crisp up more as they cool.
notes: Use raw green bananas, not ripe ones. Kerala-style with coconut oil and turmeric.

name: Sweet Potato Chips Baked
ingredients: 1/4 tsp paprika or red chilli powder
preparation: Peel and slice sweet potato very thin with a mandoline.
Toss with oil, salt, pepper, and paprika.
Arrange in a single layer on parchment-lined tray.
Bake at 170°C for 20-25 minutes, watching carefully.
Cool on the tray - they crisp up as they cool.
notes: Slice evenly for uniform cooking. They go from done to burnt very quickly.

name: Beetroot Chips Baked
preparation: Peel and slice beetroot paper-thin using a mandoline.
Toss with oil and salt.
Arrange in a single layer on parchment-lined trays.
Bake at 170°C for 20-25 minutes until crispy.
Sprinkle chaat masala and cool completely.
notes: Beautiful ruby-colored chips. Very low calorie and iron-rich.

name: Raw Mango Slices
preparation: Wash and slice raw mango into thin pieces with skin on.
Sprinkle salt, chilli powder, and black salt.
Toss gently.
Arrange on a plate.
Serve as a tangy refreshing snack.
notes: Summer seasonal treat. Raw mango is rich in vitamin C and prevents heat stroke.

name: Amla Candy
preparation: Boil amla until slightly soft. Remove seeds and cut into pieces.
Cook amla pieces with sugar on low heat until sugar coats them.
Add black salt and chaat masala.
Spread on a tray and sun-dry for 2-3 days or dehydrate.
Store in airtight container.
notes: Amla is the richest natural source of Vitamin C. Tangy sweet healthy candy.

name: Dried Amla Snack
preparation: Cut amla into pieces and discard seeds.
Sprinkle salt, turmeric, and chilli powder.
Toss well and spread on a tray.
Sun-dry for 3-4 days or dehydrate at low temperature.
Store in airtight jar - lasts months.
notes: Traditional preservation method. Great for Vitamin C boost during winters.

name: Fox Nut Kheer Snack
preparation: Roast makhana in ghee until crispy.
Grind half the makhana to a powder.
Boil milk and add makhana powder to thicken.
Add sugar and cardamom. Simmer 5 minutes.
Add whole makhana, garnish with pistachios. Serve warm or chilled.
notes: Light fasting-friendly kheer in snack portion. Low calorie and satisfying.

name: Makhana Chaat
preparation: Dry roast makhana until crispy. Cool.
Mix with onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Add chaat masala and lemon juice.
Toss well and garnish with coriander.
Top with sev and serve immediately.
notes: Crunchy, tangy and low-calorie. Serve immediately to keep the makhana crispy.

name: Makhana Raita
ingredients: 1/2 cup makhana, roasted and crushed lightly
preparation: Dry roast makhana until crispy, crush lightly.
Whisk yogurt until smooth.
Add cumin, black salt, and regular salt.
Mix in crushed makhana.
Garnish with mint and chilli powder. Serve chilled.
notes: Fasting-friendly raita. The makhana adds a unique crunch to the yogurt.

name: Oats Energy Bar Indian
preparation: Roast oats in ghee until slightly golden.
Melt jaggery on low heat.
Mix roasted oats, peanut butter, seeds, raisins, and cardamom.
Pour melted jaggery and mix quickly.
Press into a lined tray, refrigerate, and cut into bars.
notes: Indian-style energy bar with jaggery. Press firmly for bars that hold together.

name: Ragi Energy Bar
preparation: Dry roast ragi flour and oats separately.
Mix with jaggery, peanut butter, and cardamom.
Add honey to bind.
Fold in chopped nuts.
Press into a tray, refrigerate for 2 hours, and cut into bars.
notes: Calcium and iron-rich bar from ragi. Perfect pre-workout snack.

name: Multigrain Bar Indian
preparation: Roast oats, ragi flour, and wheat flour separately.
Melt jaggery with ghee.
Mix all flours, seeds, and nuts.
Pour jaggery mixture and mix well.
Press into lined tray, set in fridge, cut into bars.
notes: Three grains for balanced nutrition. Bars last a week refrigerated.

name: Sprout Salad Cup
preparation: Steam or boil sprouts lightly for 5 minutes.
Cool and mix with cucumber, tomato, and onion.
Add lemon juice, chaat masala, and salt.
Garnish with coriander.
Serve in small cups as a portable snack.
notes: Sprouting increases nutrient absorption significantly. High protein snack.

name: Moong Chaat Cup
preparation: Steam moong sprouts for 5-7 minutes. Cool.
Mix with onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Add lemon juice and chaat masala.
Garnish with sev and coriander.
Serve immediately in cups.
notes: Moong sprouts are easy to grow at home. Just soak overnight and keep moist for a day.

name: Kabuli Chana Cup
preparation: Boil kabuli chana until tender but not mushy.
Mix with onion, tomato, and green chilli.
Add tamarind chutney, chaat masala, and lemon juice.
Toss well.
Top with sev and coriander. Serve in cups.
notes: Also called chole chaat. The tamarind chutney is key for that street food taste.

name: Paneer Popcorn Baked
preparation: Cut paneer into small popcorn-sized cubes.
Mix besan, cornflour, chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala, and salt.
Coat paneer cubes in the spice mixture.
Place on greased tray and brush with oil.
Bake at 200°C for 18-20 minutes until crispy.
notes: Fun bite-sized paneer snack. The besan coating creates a crispy shell.

name: Cauliflower Popcorn Baked
preparation: Cut cauliflower into very small florets.
Mix besan, cornflour, and all spices.
Toss cauliflower in the spice coating.
Spread on greased tray and drizzle oil.
Bake at 200°C for 25 minutes until crispy and golden.
notes: Guilt-free popcorn alternative. The smaller the florets, the crunchier they get.

name: Soya Popcorn
preparation: Boil soya chunks for 5 minutes, drain and squeeze dry.
Mix besan, cornflour, chilli powder, garam masala, soy sauce, and salt.
Coat soya chunks in the mixture.
Place on greased tray and brush with oil.
Bake at 200°C for 20 minutes until crispy.
notes: Protein-packed popcorn alternative. Squeeze all water from soya for maximum crunch.

name: Jowar Puff
preparation: Heat ghee in a pan.
Add jowar puffs and toss for 1-2 minutes.
Sprinkle salt, chaat masala, and chilli powder.
Toss well to coat evenly.
Cool and store in airtight container.
notes: Jowar puffs are gluten-free and very light. Available at health food stores.

name: Ragi Puff
preparation: Heat ghee and add curry leaves until crispy.
Add ragi puffs and toss gently.
Sprinkle salt and chaat masala.
Stir for a minute on low heat.
Cool completely before storing.
notes: Ragi puffs are rich in calcium. Light and airy texture perfect for snacking.

name: Bajra Puff
preparation: Heat ghee in a pan on low flame.
Add bajra puffs and stir gently.
Sprinkle salt, pepper, and cumin powder.
Toss for 1-2 minutes.
Cool and store airtight.
notes: Bajra is iron-rich and warming. Great winter snack from Rajasthan.

name: Besan Ladoo Light
preparation: Roast besan in ghee on low heat for 12-15 minutes until golden and aromatic.
Cool the roasted besan slightly.
Add powdered jaggery and cardamom. Mix well.
Add chopped almonds.
Shape into small ladoos while still warm.
notes: Less ghee than traditional recipe. The besan must be roasted well for the right flavor.

name: Atta Ladoo Light
preparation: Roast atta in ghee on low heat for 10-12 minutes until golden.
Cool slightly and add powdered jaggery.
Mix in cardamom, nuts, and coconut.
Shape into small ladoos while still warm.
Store in airtight container.
notes: Traditional comfort snack. Uses less ghee than classic version for lighter ladoos.

name: Coconut Ladoo Light
preparation: Dry roast desiccated coconut for 3-4 minutes on low heat.
Add condensed milk and cardamom.
Cook on low heat, stirring continuously until mixture thickens.
Cool slightly until handleable.
Shape into small balls and top with pistachio.
notes: Only 3 main ingredients. Using low-fat condensed milk cuts calories.

name: Methi Mathri Baked
preparation: Crush kasuri methi and mix with flour, salt, ajwain, and chilli.
Add oil and rub into the flour.
Knead a stiff dough with minimal water.
Roll thick, cut rounds, and prick with fork.
Bake at 180°C for 20-22 minutes until crispy.
notes: Kasuri methi adds wonderful aroma. These stay crisp for weeks in airtight containers.

name: Palak Mathri Baked
preparation: Blend spinach with minimal water to make a puree.
Mix flour, salt, ajwain, and pepper.
Add oil and spinach puree. Knead stiff dough.
Roll thick and cut into shapes. Prick with fork.
Bake at 180°C for 22-25 minutes until crispy.
notes: Green-colored mathri that's more nutritious. Spinach adds iron and vitamins.

name: Ajwain Mathri Baked
preparation: Mix flour, ajwain, salt, and pepper.
Rub in oil until mixture is crumbly.
Knead into a stiff dough with water.
Roll thick and cut into shapes. Prick well with fork.
Bake at 180°C for 20 minutes until golden and crispy.
notes: Ajwain aids digestion. Classic North Indian tea-time snack.

name: Masala Papad Snack
preparation: Roast or microwave papads until puffed and crispy.
Mix onion, tomato, chilli, coriander, chaat masala, and lemon juice.
Break papads into large pieces.
Top with the masala mixture.
Serve immediately.
notes: A party favorite appetizer. Assemble just before serving to keep papads crispy.

name: Roasted Papad
preparation: Hold papad with tongs over a gas flame.
Roast evenly, turning constantly until puffed and lightly charred.
Alternatively, microwave for 30-40 seconds.
Break into pieces.
Serve as a crunchy side snack.
notes: The simplest Indian snack. Flame-roasted tastes better than microwaved.

name: Papad Chaat
preparation: Roast papads and break into bite-sized pieces.
Arrange on a plate.
Top with whisked yogurt.
Drizzle tamarind and green chutneys.
Garnish with onion, chaat masala, sev, and coriander.
notes: Quick chaat that needs no cooking. Layer just before serving.

name: Mini Uttapam Snack
preparation: Heat a tawa and grease lightly.
Pour small spoonfuls of batter to make 3-inch uttapams.
Quickly top with onion, tomato, capsicum, and chilli.
Press toppings lightly and cook until bottom is golden.
Flip and cook briefly. Serve with chutney.
notes: Mini version is perfect for snacking. Toppings can be customised to preference.

name: Rava Toast
preparation: Mix rava, yogurt, chilli, onion, coriander, and salt. Rest 10 minutes.
Spread thick layer of rava mixture on bread.
Heat oil on a tawa.
Place bread rava-side down and cook until golden and crispy.
Flip and toast other side briefly. Cut and serve.
notes: Similar to suji toast but with yogurt for extra tang. Rest the batter for fluffy texture.

name: Trail Mix Classic
preparation: Lightly roast nuts and seeds separately.
Cool completely.
Mix all ingredients in a large bowl.
Toss gently to distribute evenly.
Store in airtight containers. Portion into small bags for grab-and-go.
notes: Classic trail mix for hiking or office snacking. Calorie dense, so watch portions.

name: Tropical Trail Mix
preparation: Roast cashews and peanuts lightly.
Toast coconut chips until golden.
Cool all roasted items.
Mix with dried fruits.
Store in airtight containers.
notes: Tropical flavors in every bite. The coconut chips add a wonderful crunch.

name: Nut Berry Mix
preparation: Roast almonds and walnuts lightly.
Cool completely.
Mix with dried berries.
Add dark chocolate chips.
Store in a cool place in airtight container.
notes: Berries add antioxidants. Keep in a cool place so chocolate doesn't melt.

name: Dark Chocolate Trail Mix
preparation: Roast almonds and cashews lightly.
Cool completely.
Mix with chocolate chips, cherries, and seeds.
Add a pinch of sea salt.
Store in airtight container in a cool place.
notes: Dark chocolate has antioxidants. The salt-sweet-chocolate combo is addictive.

name: Protein Balls Peanut Butter
preparation: Mix peanut butter, oats, honey, and vanilla.
Fold in chocolate chips and flax seeds.
Refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Roll into small balls.
Store refrigerated for up to a week.
notes: No-bake energy balls. Use natural peanut butter for healthier version.

name: Energy Balls Oat
preparation: Mix oats, honey, almond butter, chia seeds, and cinnamon.
Stir until well combined.
Refrigerate for 20 minutes.
Roll into small balls.
Coat with desiccated coconut.
notes: Chia seeds add omega-3 and help bind the balls. Perfect pre-workout snack.

name: Coconut Energy Balls
preparation: Blend dates into a paste.
Mix with desiccated coconut, almond butter, honey, and cardamom.
Roll into small balls.
Coat with extra coconut.
Refrigerate until firm.
notes: Tropical flavored energy balls. Dates provide natural sweetness.

name: Almond Butter Balls
preparation: Mix almond butter, oats, honey, cocoa, and salt.
Fold in chopped almonds.
Refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Roll into balls.
Store refrigerated in airtight container.
notes: Rich chocolatey almond flavor. Almond butter is rich in vitamin E.

name: Hummus With Veggie Sticks
preparation: Scoop hummus into a serving bowl.
Sprinkle paprika and drizzle olive oil on top.
Cut all vegetables into even sticks.
Chill veggies in ice water for crunch, then pat dry.
Arrange veggies around the hummus bowl.
notes: Classic healthy snack combo. The fiber from veggies and protein from hummus keeps you full.

name: Guacamole With Chips
ingredients: baked tortilla chips or khakhra for dipping
preparation: Mash avocado with a fork - keep it chunky.
Add lime juice, onion, tomato, chilli, salt, and coriander.
Mix gently.
Serve immediately with baked chips or khakhra pieces.
Press plastic wrap directly on surface to prevent browning if storing.
notes: Rich in healthy fats. Lime juice prevents browning and adds tang.

name: Tzatziki With Pita
preparation: Grate cucumber and squeeze out all excess water.
Mix yogurt, cucumber, garlic, lemon juice, and herbs.
Season with salt and pepper.
Drizzle olive oil on top.
Cut pita into triangles and serve with the dip.
notes: Squeeze cucumber well or the dip becomes watery. Refreshing and protein-rich.

name: Rice Cakes Almond Butter
preparation: Spread almond butter evenly on rice cakes.
Top with banana slices.
Drizzle honey over the top.
Sprinkle chia seeds.
Serve immediately.
notes: Quick and satisfying snack. Rice cakes provide the crunch, almond butter the protein.

name: Rice Cakes Avocado
preparation: Mash avocado with salt, chilli flakes, and lemon juice.
Spread on rice cakes.
Top with halved cherry tomatoes.
Add extra chilli flakes if desired.
Serve immediately.
notes: Trendy healthy snack. Use ripe avocado for easy spreading.

name: Rice Cakes Honey
preparation: Spread peanut butter on rice cakes.
Drizzle honey over them.
Sprinkle a little cinnamon.
Serve as is.
Can add banana slices for extra sweetness.
notes: Sweet and satisfying. Cinnamon adds warmth and helps regulate blood sugar.

name: Apple Slices Peanut Butter
ingredients: sprinkle of granola or chopped nuts
preparation: Wash and slice apple into wedges.
Spread peanut butter on each slice or serve as a dip.
Drizzle honey if desired.
Sprinkle granola or chopped nuts.
Serve immediately to prevent browning.
notes: Classic healthy snack. Rub lemon on apple slices to prevent browning.

name: Celery Peanut Butter
preparation: Wash celery stalks and cut to desired length.
Fill the groove of each stalk with peanut butter.
Top with raisins.
Arrange on a plate.
Serve chilled.
notes: Known as 'ants on a log' - fun snack for kids and adults alike.

name: Banana Almond Butter
preparation: Peel and slice banana into rounds.
Spread almond butter on half the slices.
Top with remaining banana slices to make sandwiches.
Drizzle honey and sprinkle chia seeds.
Serve immediately.
notes: Banana provides potassium, almond butter provides healthy fats. Quick energy booster.

name: Greek Yogurt With Honey
preparation: Spoon yogurt into a bowl.
Drizzle honey on top.
Sprinkle chopped nuts.
Add pomegranate seeds for color and crunch.
Serve chilled.
notes: High in protein from Greek yogurt. Great post-workout snack.

name: Yogurt Granola Cup
preparation: Layer yogurt in a cup or glass.
Add a layer of granola.
Top with mixed berries.
Drizzle honey.
Finish with sliced almonds.
notes: Layer just before eating to keep granola crunchy. A balanced snack with protein, carbs, and fats.

name: Skyr Berry Cup
ingredients: 1 cup skyr or thick strained yogurt
preparation: Spoon skyr into a serving cup.
Top with mixed berries.
Drizzle honey.
Sprinkle flax seeds.
Garnish with fresh mint.
notes: Skyr has more protein than regular yogurt. Can substitute with hung curd.

name: Popcorn Air Popped
ingredients: spray butter or 1 tsp melted butter
preparation: Pop kernels in an air popper or microwave in a paper bag.
Spread popcorn on a tray.
Spray or drizzle minimal butter.
Sprinkle salt evenly.
Toss gently and serve.
notes: One of the lowest calorie snacks. Air popping avoids the oil of stovetop popping.

name: Seasoned Popcorn Light
preparation: Pop kernels using air popper or stovetop with minimal oil.
Drizzle melted butter.
Mix garlic powder, herbs, salt, and pepper.
Sprinkle seasoning over popcorn.
Toss gently to coat evenly.
notes: Italian herb popcorn feels gourmet but is super simple. Customise seasoning to taste.

name: Parmesan Popcorn
preparation: Pop kernels using air popper or stovetop.
Drizzle melted butter while popcorn is hot.
Sprinkle Parmesan cheese evenly.
Add garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Toss well so cheese sticks to the popcorn.
notes: Add Parmesan while popcorn is hot so it sticks. Cheesy umami flavor.

name: Veggie Sticks Ranch Light
preparation: Mix yogurt, dill, garlic powder, onion powder, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Refrigerate dip for 15 minutes for flavors to meld.
Cut all vegetables into even sticks.
Chill veggies in ice water, then drain.
Serve veggies with the yogurt ranch dip.
notes: Yogurt-based ranch is much lighter than store-bought. High volume, low calorie snack.

name: Cucumber Hummus Bites
preparation: Slice cucumber into thick rounds (about 1cm).
Pipe or spoon a dollop of hummus on each slice.
Top with a cherry tomato half.
Sprinkle paprika.
Garnish with fresh dill.
notes: Elegant party snack that's very low calorie. Make just before serving.

name: Carrot Sticks Hummus
preparation: Peel and cut carrots into even sticks.
Chill in ice water for 10 minutes for extra crunch.
Drain and pat dry.
Serve with hummus in a small bowl.
Drizzle olive oil and paprika on the hummus.
notes: Carrots are rich in beta-carotene. Classic healthy office snack.

name: Edamame Snack
preparation: Boil edamame in salted water for 4-5 minutes.
Drain well.
Sprinkle sea salt and chilli flakes.
Toss well.
Serve warm - squeeze pods to pop out the beans.
notes: High protein plant-based snack. Available frozen at most supermarkets.

name: Roasted Edamame
preparation: Toss shelled edamame with olive oil, salt, garlic powder, and paprika.
Spread on a baking tray in single layer.
Bake at 190°C for 30-35 minutes until crunchy.
Shake tray halfway through.
Cool completely for maximum crunch.
notes: Crunchy high-protein snack. They shrink a lot during roasting, so make a big batch.

name: Wasabi Edamame
preparation: Cook edamame until tender, drain well.
Mix wasabi paste, soy sauce, and sesame oil.
Toss edamame in the wasabi mixture.
Sprinkle salt and sesame seeds.
Serve warm or chilled.
notes: Spicy Japanese-inspired snack. Adjust wasabi to your heat tolerance.

name: Mixed Nuts Unsalted
preparation: Lightly roast each type of nut separately for even cooking.
Cool completely.
Mix all nuts together.
Store in an airtight container.
Portion into small servings for grab-and-go.
notes: Unsalted nuts are heart-healthier. A small handful provides healthy fats and protein.

name: Roasted Almonds
preparation: Toss almonds with olive oil and salt.
Spread on a baking tray.
Roast at 170°C for 12-15 minutes, stirring halfway.
Cool on the tray.
Store in airtight container.
notes: Almonds are rich in vitamin E and magnesium. Best snack nut for heart health.

name: Cashew Clusters Light
preparation: Toss cashews with honey, cinnamon, salt, and coconut oil.
Spread in clumps on a parchment-lined tray.
Bake at 170°C for 12-15 minutes until golden.
Press clusters together gently while still warm.
Cool completely before breaking into clusters.
notes: The honey creates natural clusters. Don't separate while warm or they'll crumble.

name: Cheese And Crackers Light
preparation: Arrange crackers on a plate.
Place a slice of cheese on each cracker.
Top with a cherry tomato half.
Garnish with fresh basil.
Serve immediately.
notes: Use aged cheese for more flavor with less quantity. Simple elegant snack.

name: Whole Wheat Crackers
preparation: Mix flour, salt, pepper, and rosemary.
Add olive oil and mix until crumbly.
Add water gradually to form a stiff dough.
Roll out very thin and cut into squares.
Bake at 180°C for 12-15 minutes until crispy.
notes: Homemade crackers beat store-bought in freshness. Roll as thin as possible.

name: Seed Crackers
preparation: Mix all seeds with water and salt. Let sit 15 minutes until gel forms.
Spread very thin on a parchment-lined tray.
Bake at 160°C for 25-30 minutes until crispy.
Score into squares while still warm.
Cool completely and break apart.
notes: Grain-free crackers. The flax seeds create a natural gel that holds everything together.

name: Fruit Leather Homemade
ingredients: 2 cups ripe mango or mixed fruit puree
preparation: Blend ripe fruit into smooth puree.
Add honey and lemon juice.
Spread very thin on a parchment-lined tray.
Dehydrate at 70°C for 6-8 hours or sun-dry.
Cut into strips and roll up.
notes: Natural fruit candy with no preservatives. Mango leather is especially popular in India.

name: Dried Mango
preparation: Peel and slice mangoes into thin strips.
Toss with lemon juice.
Arrange on dehydrator trays or oven rack.
Dehydrate at 65°C for 8-10 hours.
Sprinkle chaat masala if desired. Store airtight.
notes: Concentrated mango sweetness. Sun-drying works too in hot climates.

name: Dried Cranberry Mix
preparation: Use ready dried cranberries.
Lightly roast almonds and pumpkin seeds.
Cool completely.
Mix all ingredients together.
Store in an airtight container.
notes: Sweet-tart cranberries with chocolate and nuts. Great desk snack.

name: Granola Bar Homemade
preparation: Mix oats, nuts, dried fruit, and chocolate chips.
Warm honey and peanut butter together.
Add vanilla and salt to the wet mixture.
Combine wet and dry ingredients.
Press firmly into a lined tray and bake at 170°C for 20 minutes. Cool and cut.
notes: Press very firmly for bars that hold together. Cheaper and healthier than store-bought.

name: Oat Bar
preparation: Mix oats, seeds, cinnamon, and salt.
Warm honey and coconut oil together.
Combine with oat mixture.
Press firmly into a lined tray.
Bake at 170°C for 18-20 minutes. Cool completely before cutting.
notes: Simple oat bar with minimal ingredients. The coconut oil sets firm when cooled.

name: Date Nut Bar
preparation: Blend dates until they form a sticky paste.
Pulse almonds and cashews into coarse pieces.
Mix date paste with nuts, oats, salt, and cardamom.
Press firmly into a lined tray.
Refrigerate for 2 hours and cut into bars.
notes: No-bake bar sweetened only with dates. Larabar-style with Indian touch.

name: Cottage Cheese Berries
ingredients: 1/2 cup low-fat cottage cheese (paneer crumbled or ricotta)
preparation: Spoon cottage cheese into a bowl.
Top with mixed berries.
Drizzle honey.
Sprinkle chopped walnuts.
Serve chilled.
notes: High protein, low calorie snack. Can use crumbled fresh paneer as cottage cheese.

name: Cream Cheese Celery Light
ingredients: 1/4 tsp everything bagel seasoning or chaat masala
preparation: Wash and trim celery stalks.
Fill each stalk groove with cream cheese.
Sprinkle seasoning on top.
Add dried cranberries.
Cut into bite-sized pieces and serve.
notes: Light cream cheese keeps it under 120 calories. Crunchy and creamy contrast.

name: Turkey Roll Ups
preparation: Lay turkey slices flat.
Spread thin layer of cream cheese.
Place a lettuce leaf on each slice.
Add cucumber sticks.
Roll up tightly and secure with toothpicks.
notes: Low-carb, high-protein snack. Keep refrigerated until serving.

name: Ham Cheese Roll Light
preparation: Lay ham slices flat.
Cut cheese slices in half and place on ham.
Add lettuce and a thin spread of mustard.
Add pickle slices if desired.
Roll up tightly and secure with toothpicks.
notes: Quick no-cook high-protein snack. Use lean ham for fewer calories.

name: Baked Kale Chips
preparation: Wash kale and dry thoroughly - this is crucial.
Tear into bite-sized pieces.
Toss with olive oil, salt, and garlic powder.
Spread in a single layer on baking trays.
Bake at 150°C for 15-18 minutes until crispy but not burnt.
notes: Must be completely dry before baking or they'll steam instead of crisp. Watch carefully.

name: Sweet Potato Fries Baked
preparation: Cut sweet potato into even-sized fries.
Toss with oil, salt, paprika, garlic powder, and pepper.
Arrange in single layer on baking tray - don't overcrowd.
Bake at 200°C for 25-30 minutes, flipping halfway.
Serve hot with ketchup or yogurt dip.
notes: Don't overcrowd the tray or they'll steam. Space between fries = crispier fries.

name: Zucchini Chips Baked
preparation: Slice zucchini into thin rounds.
Mix breadcrumbs, Parmesan, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Brush zucchini slices with oil.
Dip in breadcrumb mixture.
Bake at 200°C for 20-25 minutes until golden and crispy.
notes: Salt and drain zucchini slices first to remove moisture for crispier chips.

name: Protein Shake Snack
preparation: Add milk, protein powder, banana, and peanut butter to a blender.
Add ice cubes.
Blend until smooth and frothy.
Pour into a glass.
Serve immediately.
notes: Great post-workout snack. The banana adds natural sweetness and creaminess.

name: Smoothie Cup Small
preparation: Blend banana, berries, yogurt, milk, and honey until smooth.
Pour into a small bowl or cup.
Top with granola.
Serve immediately.
Can add chia seeds or sliced fruit on top.
notes: Smoothie bowl in snack size. Eat with a spoon for a more filling experience.

name: Green Juice
preparation: Wash all greens thoroughly.
Chop apple and cucumber into pieces.
Blend spinach, cucumber, apple, ginger, and water.
Strain through a fine sieve or cheesecloth.
Add lemon juice and serve fresh.
notes: Drink immediately as nutrients oxidize quickly. The apple balances the green taste.

name: Chia Pudding Snack
preparation: Mix chia seeds with milk, honey, and vanilla.
Stir well to prevent clumping.
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Top with fresh fruit.
Serve chilled.
notes: Prep the night before for a grab-and-go snack. Chia seeds expand to 10x their size.

name: Overnight Oats Snack Cup
preparation: Mix oats, milk, yogurt, honey, and chia seeds.
Pour into a small jar.
Refrigerate overnight or at least 4 hours.
Top with fresh fruit before eating.
Enjoy cold straight from the fridge.
notes: Snack-sized portion of overnight oats. Perfect for mid-afternoon energy.

name: Muesli Cup Small
preparation: Layer yogurt in a small cup.
Add muesli on top.
Drizzle honey.
Top with berries and almonds.
Serve immediately for crunchy muesli, or let sit for softer texture.
notes: Quick parfait-style snack. Use unsweetened muesli to control sugar intake.

name: Bruschetta Light
ingredients: 2 slices whole wheat baguette or bread
preparation: Toast bread slices until golden and crispy.
Rub a cut garlic clove on the warm toast.
Mix diced tomatoes with basil, olive oil, balsamic, salt, and pepper.
Spoon tomato mixture on toast.
Serve immediately.
notes: Italian classic. Use ripe tomatoes at room temperature for the best flavor.

name: Caprese Skewers
preparation: Thread a basil leaf, cherry tomato, and mozzarella ball on each skewer.
Arrange on a plate.
Drizzle with olive oil and balsamic glaze.
Season with salt and pepper.
Serve at room temperature.
notes: Elegant no-cook party snack. Bring mozzarella to room temperature for best flavor.

name: Olive Tapenade Crackers
preparation: Blend olives, garlic, capers, olive oil, and lemon juice.
Pulse until chunky - don't over-blend.
Spread tapenade on crackers.
Garnish with a tiny olive or herb.
Serve immediately.
notes: Mediterranean snack rich in healthy fats. Tapenade keeps for a week refrigerated.

name: Stuffed Dates
ingredients: 2 tbsp almond butter or peanut butter
preparation: Slit dates lengthwise and remove pits.
Fill each date with a small amount of nut butter.
Press an almond or walnut half into each.
Sprinkle with sea salt.
Serve at room temperature or slightly chilled.
notes: Natural candy with protein. Sweet, salty, and satisfying in just two bites.

name: Medjool Date Walnut
preparation: Slit dates and remove pits.
Lightly warm ghee and mix with cardamom.
Brush inside of dates with cardamom ghee.
Stuff each date with a walnut half.
Press closed and serve.
notes: Indian-style stuffed dates. Cardamom and ghee add a traditional touch.

name: Chocolate Date Snack Light
preparation: Pit dates and stuff with any nut butter if desired.
Dip bottom half in melted dark chocolate.
Place on parchment paper.
Sprinkle pistachios and sea salt.
Refrigerate until chocolate sets.
notes: Feels like a dessert but is just dates and dark chocolate. Satisfy sweet cravings naturally.

name: Seaweed Snack
preparation: Open pack of roasted seaweed sheets.
They're ready to eat as-is from the pack.
For extra flavor, lightly brush with sesame oil.
Sprinkle a tiny bit of salt.
Eat as crispy sheets or crumble over rice.
notes: Extremely low calorie and rich in iodine. Great for thyroid health.

name: Nori Rolls Mini
preparation: Mix cooked rice with rice vinegar.
Place nori sheet on a bamboo mat.
Spread thin layer of rice on nori.
Add cucumber and avocado at one end.
Roll tightly, cut into bite-sized pieces. Serve with soy sauce.
notes: Simplified sushi rolls for snacking. Wet your hands when handling sushi rice.

name: Sushi Bites
preparation: Season rice with vinegar, sugar, and salt.
Wet hands and shape rice into small oval mounds.
Wrap each with a thin nori strip.
Top with a slice of avocado or cucumber.
Serve with soy sauce and wasabi.
notes: Nigiri-style bites. Simple vegetarian sushi that anyone can make at home.

name: Avocado Deviled Eggs
preparation: Cut boiled eggs in half and remove yolks.
Mash yolks with avocado, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Pipe or spoon the filling back into egg whites.
Sprinkle paprika.
Garnish with chives and serve chilled.
notes: Avocado replaces mayo for healthier fats. The green color is beautiful.

name: Hard Boiled Egg Snack
preparation: Place eggs in cold water and bring to a boil.
Once boiling, turn off heat and cover for 10 minutes.
Transfer to ice water to cool.
Peel and cut in half.
Season with salt, pepper, and chaat masala.
notes: The perfect hard-boiled egg: start cold, boil, rest 10 min, ice bath. No green ring.

name: Egg Muffin Mini
ingredients: 1/4 cup mixed vegetables (capsicum, onion, tomato), chopped
preparation: Whisk eggs with salt, pepper, and herbs.
Grease a mini muffin tin with cooking spray.
Distribute chopped vegetables in each muffin cup.
Pour egg mixture over vegetables.
Bake at 180°C for 15 minutes until set. Top with cheese in last 2 minutes.
notes: Meal prep friendly - make a batch and refrigerate. Reheat for 30 seconds.

name: Banana Oat Cookies
preparation: Mash bananas thoroughly.
Mix in oats, chocolate chips, cinnamon, and honey.
Drop spoonfuls onto a lined baking tray.
Flatten slightly with the back of a spoon.
Bake at 175°C for 15-18 minutes until golden.
notes: Only 2 main ingredients! Ripe bananas replace sugar, eggs, and butter.

name: Almond Flour Cookies
ingredients: 2 tbsp chocolate chips or chopped nuts
preparation: Mix almond flour, honey, vanilla, and salt.
Fold in chocolate chips or nuts.
Shape into small flat cookies.
Place on a lined baking tray.
Bake at 170°C for 10-12 minutes until edges are golden.
notes: Gluten-free cookies. Almond flour makes them naturally moist and rich.

name: Coconut Macaroons Light
ingredients: 2 egg whites or 3 tbsp condensed milk
preparation: Mix coconut, sugar, vanilla, and salt.
Add egg whites (or condensed milk for eggless version) and mix well.
Shape into small mounds on a lined tray.
Bake at 160°C for 18-20 minutes until tops are golden.
Cool on tray before removing.
notes: Naturally gluten-free. The condensed milk version is eggless and easier.

name: Frozen Yogurt Bites
preparation: Mix yogurt with honey.
Drop small spoonfuls on a parchment-lined tray.
Press a berry or two into each dollop.
Sprinkle nuts or sprinkles on top.
Freeze for 2-3 hours until solid. Store in freezer bag.
notes: Healthy frozen treat. Let sit 2 minutes before eating so they're not rock hard.

name: Frozen Banana Bites
ingredients: 2 tbsp chopped peanuts or coconut flakes
preparation: Slice bananas into thick rounds.
Insert a toothpick in each.
Dip halfway in melted dark chocolate.
Sprinkle with chopped peanuts or coconut.
Freeze on parchment-lined tray for 2 hours.
notes: Tastes like a frozen dessert but it's just banana and dark chocolate. Kids love these.

name: Frozen Grape Snack
preparation: Wash grapes and remove from stems.
Pat dry completely.
Toss with lime juice if desired.
Spread on a tray in single layer.
Freeze for 3-4 hours. Transfer to a freezer bag.
notes: Nature's candy when frozen. Pop them like candy on hot days.

name: Protein Muffin Mini
preparation: Mash banana and mix with egg, yogurt, and honey.
Add oat flour, protein powder, baking powder, and salt.
Mix until just combined - don't overmix.
Pour into a greased mini muffin tin.
Bake at 175°C for 12-15 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.
notes: High protein snack muffins. Make a batch on Sunday for the week.

name: Bran Muffin Mini
preparation: Soak wheat bran in milk for 10 minutes.
Mix egg, honey, and yogurt.
Combine with bran mixture.
Add flour, baking soda, cinnamon, and raisins.
Pour into mini muffin tin and bake at 175°C for 15 minutes.
notes: High fiber muffins for digestive health. Soaking bran softens it and improves texture.

name: Zucchini Muffin Mini
preparation: Grate zucchini and squeeze out all moisture.
Mix egg, honey, oil, and vanilla.
Add flour, baking powder, cinnamon, and salt.
Fold in squeezed zucchini.
Pour into greased mini muffin tin and bake at 175°C for 15-18 minutes.
notes: Hidden veggie muffin. You can't taste the zucchini - it just adds moisture.
